CHIEF COMPLAINT: Fever

PRESENT ILLNESS: ___ yo female with a history of cholangiocarcinoma who is  admitted with fevers likely due to infected hepatic fluid  collections. The patient states she had been feeling fatigued  since she was recently discharged but overall well until  yesterday when she developed a fever of 101 and generalized  malaise. She has had some ongoing "soreness" in her abdomen which she attributes to  the recent biliary drains. The fevers and malaise continued  today so she came to the ED. She was recently admitted from  ___ and had 3 PTBD drains which were then converted to  internal stents which was complicated by pancreatitis. Her  course was also complicated by pulmonary edema. When she was  discharged she had a known hepatic fluid collection but given a lack of symptoms at  the time the decision was made to defer treatment until later as  an outpatient. Since discharged she reports some intermittent  nausea but denies any diarrhea or dysuria or other new symptoms.

MEDICAL HISTORY: PAST ONCOLOGIC HISTORY: Cholangiocarcinoma - Patient initially noted to have liver function abnormalities  during routine PCP blood draw. Subequent US noted biliary  dilatation and MRI with evidence of intrahepatic biliary  obstruction. CA ___ elevated at 319, CEA 3.4. Patient underwent  ERCP with Dr. ___ at ___ and had malignant  appearing stricture but non-diagnostic cytology with  post-procedure pancreatitis. Further workup with CT guided liver  bx on ___ ___onsistent with  moderately-differentiated adnosarcoma possibly consistent with  cholangiocarcinoma.

MEDICATION ON ADMISSION: The Preadmission Medication list is accurate and complete. 1. Amlodipine 10 mg PO DAILY  2. Ondansetron 8 mg PO Q8H:PRN nausea  3. Ca cmb ___ 120-1,000-10 mg-unit-mg oral  Q24H  4. Lactobacillus acidophilus 1 billion cell oral DAILY  5. Aspirin 81 mg PO DAILY

ALLERGIES: Sulfa (Sulfonamide Antibiotics) / furosemide / latex

PHYSICAL EXAM: ADMISSION PHYSICAL EXAM: VITAL SIGNS: T 99 BP 118/50 HR 86 O2 91%RA GENERAL:  NAD HEENT:  MMM, no OP lesions CV:  RR, NL S1S2 PULM:  CTAB ABD:  Soft, ND, no masses or hepatosplenomegaly, mild tenderness to palpation RUQ. LIMBS:  No edema, clubbing, tremors, or asterixis SKIN:  No rashes or skin breakdown NEURO: Alert and oriented, no focal deficits.

FAMILY HISTORY: The patient's mother died at ___ years.  Her father died at ___  years of an MI.  One sister died in her ___, another sister and  three children are living.  Her son was diagnosed with prostate  cancer at ___ years.

SOCIAL HISTORY: ___ Family History: The patient's mother died at ___ years.  Her father died at ___  years of an MI.  One sister died in her ___, another sister and  three children are living.  Her son was diagnosed with prostate  cancer at ___ years.

CHIEF COMPLAINT: Rectal bleeding

PRESENT ILLNESS: Ms. ___ is a ___ female with history of Afib  s/p watchman procedure, COPD on 2L home O2, multiple myeloma and recent admission for cellulitis requiring I and D who presents  as transfer from ___ with concern for GI bleed.   The patient reports that over the past few weeks she has been experience indigestion after she eats. She feels like she will start eating and then get pain. Then the day prior to admission, she one episode of dark black stool. She had associated fatigue and shortness of breath. She called her PCP who advised she come to the emergency department for evaluation.  The patient was admitted to ___ ___. She was seen by GI and noted to have downtrending HCT. Given concern that she may need enteroscopy, she was transferred to ___.   While at ___ she was on  an IV PPI. She received 1unit of PRBCs, Lasix 40mg IV and had a second unit of blood running which was stopped on arrival.   Currently, the patient reports she feels well. She has no abdominal pain,  no nausea or vomiting. She denies feeling lightheaded or dizzy. No shortness of breath. She reports her last BM was yesterday.  The patient was recently admitted to ___ ___ to ___ for cellulitis/lymphangitis of her finger.  Following that admission, she was discharged on a ___ line with cefazolin for 14 days (PICC was removed ___ followed by oral antibiotics with dicloxacillin 500mg QID with plan to continue until ID follow up on ___.

MEDICAL HISTORY: A. fib status post watchman procedure COPD on Chronic home O2 at 2 L Hyperlipidemia Osteoarthritis Anemia Dilated and ICM, diastolic dysfunction, tachycardia induced cardiomyopathy, EF 20 to 25% by echo in ___, improved to 60% in ___ Type II DM Hypertension Multiple myeloma stage III started on Velcade in ___ GI bleed  due to AVMs Colon polyps Gout ?  Pulmonary toxicity related to amiodarone Status post appendectomy Status post hemorrhoidectomy

MEDICATION ON ADMISSION: The Preadmission Medication list is accurate and complete. 1. Acetaminophen 650 mg PO Q6H:PRN Pain - Mild/Fever  2. Torsemide 80 mg PO DAILY  3. GlipiZIDE 5 mg PO DAILY  4. Simvastatin 10 mg PO QPM  5. Allopurinol ___ mg PO DAILY  6. MetFORMIN (Glucophage) 1000 mg PO BID  7. budesonide-formoterol 160-4.5 mcg/actuation inhalation BID  8. SITagliptin 25 mg oral DAILY  9. Acyclovir 400 mg PO DAILY  10. Metoprolol Succinate XL 25 mg PO DAILY  11. DiCLOXacillin 500 mg PO Q6H

ALLERGIES: Erythromycin Base / Cardizem / amiodarone

PHYSICAL EXAM: ADMISSION EXAM: VITALS: 97.9 BP:108/62 HR:94 R:18 O2:95 2L   GENERAL: Alert and in no apparent distress, well appearing EYES: Anicteric, pupils equally round ENT: Ears and nose without visible erythema, masses, or trauma.  Oropharynx without visible lesion, erythema or exudate CV: Heart irregular with ___ systolic murmur, no S3, no S4.  No JVD. RESP: Lungs clear to auscultation with good air movement bilaterally.  Breathing is non-labored GI: Abdomen soft, non-distended, non-tender to palpation.  Bowel sounds present.  No HSM MSK: Neck supple, moves all extremities, strength grossly full and symmetric bilaterally in all limbs SKIN: No rashes or ulcerations noted. Faint venous stasis  changes of lower extremities. NEURO: Alert, oriented, face symmetric, gaze conjugate with  EOMI, speech fluent, moves all limbs, sensation to light touch grossly intact throughout PSYCH: pleasant, appropriate affect

<think>
Okay, let's tackle this. The user wants me to extract all relevant diagnoses from the admission note and assign the most appropriate ICD-10 codes. They mentioned that usually patients have 3-30 codes, so I need to be thorough but not overdo it.

First, I'll start by reading through the admission note carefully. The chief complaint is rectal bleeding, and the present illness mentions a history of several conditions. Let's go through each section.

In the present illness, the patient has a history of Afib (atrial fibrillation) post Watchman procedure. That's I48. Then COPD on 2L home O2, which is J44.9. They also mention type 2 diabetes mellitus, so E11.9. Hypertension is noted, so I10. Multiple myeloma stage III, which is C88.9. Recent cellulitis requiring I&D, so L03.9. The patient had a GI bleed due to AVMs, so K25.9. Anemia is mentioned in the medical history, so D64.9. Diastolic dysfunction and tachycardia-induced cardiomyopathy, which would be I50.9. Hyperlipidemia is E78.9. Gout is M10.9. The patient is on a PICC line, so T88.9.

The patient was referred to colleagues of Breast and Plastic Surgery to discuss a surgical procedure but it appears that patient declined intervention at that time and agreed on follow up with repeated imaging.   On ___ another mammogram and ultrasound showed further enlargement of her left breast mass, now measuring 5.1 x 3.8 x 4.3 cm. The axilla appeared normal and without adenopathy.
